title: 2022 FOMC Press Releases
summary: This is a navigation/index page listing all FOMC press releases from 2022 on the Federal Reserve website. The page contains 15 dated entries including 8 FOMC statements and 4 sets of economic projections. No substantive policy content is present—this index page only provides links to individual press releases.
key_points: This is a listing page containing dates and titles of 2022 FOMC events, not the actual policy statements or projections.
2022 FOMC statements were released on: January 26, March 16, May 4, June 15, July 27, September 21, November 2, December 14.
Economic projections were released following March 15-16, June 14-15, September 20-21, and December 13-14 FOMC meetings.
The page also references a February 18, 2022 FOMC adoption of new investment and trading rules.
A 'Plans for Reducing the Size of the Federal Reserve's Balance Sheet' was released May 4, 2022.
Page last updated February 26, 2026, but content is historical from 2022.
why_it_matters: This index page serves as a reference guide to 2022 Fed policy communications but contains no actionable policy content. Researchers must navigate to individual press releases for actual statements and projection details.
macro_context: This page indexes 2022 FOMC communications when the Fed aggressively tightened monetary policy, raising rates from 0-0.25% in March 2022 to 4.25-4.50% by December 2022.

issues_found: Source is a listing/index page containing only dates and titles of press releases - no substantive policy content
event_type 'fomc_statement' is misapplied; the source does not contain any FOMC statements, only references to them
Per core rule #5: listing_page source type must be rejected
Per core rule #6: container pages and thin low-signal sources bias toward reject
No actionable policy content exists in this source for research archive purposes
Historical content from 2022 (over 3 years old) - while individual FOMC events are significant, this index page has no standalone archival value

notes: SOURCE CLASSIFICATION: Non-archiveable listing page. The source is a Federal Reserve website index of 2022 FOMC press releases. Per core rule #6, listing pages should be treated as non-archiveable. The page contains only dates, titles, and category labels - no actual policy content. While the indexed 2022 events are historically significant (rapid Fed tightening cycle), this particular source provides no substantive material for a research archive. Recommend archiving individual FOMC statements and projections directly rather than this index. Historical record from 2022 (over 3 years prior to TODAY).
